guideline_references: ANZCOR Guideline 9.2.4 (Seizures / status epilepticus first aid)
Epilepsy Society / ILAE operational definition of status epilepticus (>5 min continuous convulsive activity)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who administered IM midazolam and manually maintained the airway; the AI Doctor CANNOT inject a benzodiazepine or manage the airway physically, and is scored on recognising a >5-minute continuous convulsion as a time-critical emergency, calling 000 immediately, and coaching the carer through positioning and (only if the patient has one) their own prescribed rescue-benzodiazepine action plan.
Age inferred as adult (~45) from a known epilepsy history on maintenance levetiracetam — no age is stated in the source — flagged.
Precipitant is anti-seizure medication withdrawal (ran out of levetiracetam 3 days ago) — tests whether the system elicits the adherence lapse without anchoring on it.
Fever 38.5C is most likely exertional/hyperthermic from sustained muscle activity, but CNS infection is an important-not-to-miss competing cause — tests premature closure.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-047.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
